macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Futures Market Liquidations Reach $332 Million in 24 Hours
items.summary: The cryptocurrency derivatives market saw $332 million in liquidations over the past 24 hours, led by Bitcoin at $187 million and Ethereum at $83.57 million, with Solana and XRP also heavily impacted amid high volatility.

items.title: Bitcoin Plunges Below $75,000 as Strait of Hormuz Closes
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped below $75,000 following the full closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Iran-US negotiation breakdown, with the Fear & Greed Index hitting Extreme Fear at 21 and $237 million in long BTC positions liquidated in 24 hours.

items.title: Ethereum Falls Below $2,300 with 2.87% Drop in 24 Hours
items.summary: Ethereum price declined below 2,300 USDT, marking a 2.87% decrease over the past 24 hours amid broader market downturn.

items.title: KelpDAO Suspends rsETH Contracts After Suspicious Cross-Chain Activity
items.summary: KelpDAO halted rsETH contracts on Ethereum mainnet and Layer 2 networks due to detected suspicious activity, launching an investigation with LayerZero and security experts.

items.title: Aave Token Drops Over 18% Amid KelpDAO Vulnerability Sell-Off
items.summary: Aave experienced an 18% price decline as major investors sold off holdings following a vulnerability in KelpDAO.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Releases Mythos AI Model, Sparks Finance Sector Alarms
items.summary: Anthropic launched Mythos, a new AI model in limited release, which UK researchers demonstrated can exploit systems with weak security, prompting global finance leaders to warn it could disrupt banking infrastructure.

items.title: GPT-5.4 Surpasses Human Baseline on Desktop Productivity Tasks
items.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.4 achieved a 75% score on real-world desktop productivity tasks, exceeding the human baseline of 72.4%, amid reports of OpenAI reaching 25 billion in annualized revenue.
items.source: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones

items.title: AI Layoffs Accelerate with Atlassian Cutting 1,600 Jobs
items.summary: Atlassian laid off 1,600 employees (10% of workforce) while announcing 800 new AI-related hires; broader trends show AI job postings up 340% since 2024 as traditional roles decline.
items.source: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones

items.title: Block Lays Off Half Its Workforce for AI Integration
items.summary: Block laid off half its company to pivot toward AI, but reports indicate AI has not yet met expectations in replacing the roles.
items.source: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones

items.title: China's Humanoid Robots Race in Beijing Half Marathon
items.summary: Around 20 humanoid robots, including winner Tiangong Ultra (2:40:42 finish), participated in a Beijing half marathon, testing adaptation to public environments like crowds and uneven terrain.

items.title: Panthalassa Proposes Ocean-Powered Data Centers for AI
items.summary: Renewable energy firm Panthalassa unveiled sea-based data centers powered by ocean waves to address the massive energy demands of AI infrastructure and reduce carbon pollution.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Crimson Desert Receives Massive Content Update with Huge Changes
items.summary: Crimson Desert is getting its biggest update yet, featuring significant changes after 300 hours of play, with sales reaching 5 million units and nearly 100k concurrent players.

items.title: Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 Update Adds New Weapons and Free Bundle
items.summary: Fortnite's latest update introduces a new pistol revolver, sweeper shotgun, cube weapons, and a free bundle available for 24 hours, with an upcoming Overwatch collab featuring Tracer, D.Va, Mercy, and Genji skins.

items.title: Indie Game Trailer Banned at BAFTAs for 'Triggering' Content Amid AAA Decline
items.summary: An indie game trailer was banned at the BAFTAs over triggering content, as discussions highlight AAA games struggling while indies and AA titles like Crimson Desert succeed.

items.title: News Tower Full Release Now Available
items.summary: News Tower has launched its full release, allowing players to manage a 1930s New York City newspaper, defining editorial lines and sourcing global stories.

items.title: Neo Geo Potentially Reborn with New Retro Hardware News
items.summary: Recent retro gaming news covers potential Neo Geo AES+ revival, Anbernic RG Rotate confirmation, and other throwback developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: SK hynix Begins Mass Production of 192GB SOCAMM2 Memory for Nvidia's Vera Rubin
items.summary: SK hynix has started mass production of a next-gen LPDDR5X low-power DRAM module designed for Nvidia's Vera Rubin AI servers.

items.title: Tesla Launches Robotaxi Service in Houston and Dallas with Near-Zero Availability
items.summary: Tesla expanded its Robotaxi service to Houston and Dallas, but deployments show 0% to 2% availability over the past 24 hours with only brief spikes.

items.title: Blue Origin Reuses New Glenn Booster for First Time in Florida Launch
items.summary: Blue Origin successfully reused and recovered a New Glenn rocket booster during a Florida launch, advancing reusable launch technology.

items.title: NASA Shuts Off Key Instrument on Voyager 1 to Extend Mission
items.summary: NASA deactivated a key instrument on Voyager 1 to conserve power and keep the first interstellar explorer operational longer.

items.title: Vestas Completes Installation on SunZia Wind Farm, Now Online
items.summary: Vestas finished installing turbines at Pattern Energy’s SunZia, the largest clean energy project in the US, which has now come online.

items.title: Apple's WWDC Invite Teases Revamped Siri; Mac Launches Delayed by Memory Shortages
items.summary: Apple's WWDC 2026 invite hints at a new Siri interface for iOS 27, while memory shortages may delay Mac Studio and touch MacBook Pro launches.
